title: API Keys - Access iMash Programmatically
lede: API keys let your own software talk to iMash through the REST API — start calls, read call logs, sync CRM records, and more — without logging into the dashboard. This guide covers generating, storing, and revoking keys.

steps.title: What API keys are for
steps.content:
An API key is a secret token that identifies your account when you call the iMash REST API. Instead of a username and password, your code sends the key with each request.

  • Use them to automate iMash from your own backend, scripts, or third-party tools.
  • Each key acts on behalf of your account, so treat it like a password.
  • A key is shown in full only once, at the moment you create it. After that, iMash stores only a hashed version and can show you the prefix.

images.caption: The API Keys page lists your keys with their name, prefix, created date, and last used time.
The API Keys page lists your keys with their name, prefix, created date, and last used time.

steps.title: Open the API Keys page
steps.content:
1. In the left-hand navigation, open Account.
  2. Click API Keys.
  3. You will see a table of your existing keys, each showing its Name, Key Prefix, Created At, and Last Used.

steps.title: Generate a new key
steps.content:
1. Click Generate New API Key in the top-right.
  2. Enter a clear, descriptive name (for example "Zapier integration" or "Nightly CRM sync") so you can tell your keys apart later.
  3. Click Generate.
  4. iMash displays the full secret key. Use the eye icon to reveal it and the Copy button to copy it.

steps.title: Copy and store it safely
steps.content:
The full key is shown once and cannot be retrieved again. If you lose it, you must generate a new one.

  • Paste it straight into your integration's secret store or an environment variable — never into shared documents or chat.
  • Do not embed the key in front-end code, mobile apps, or anything a user can view.
  • Give each integration its own key so you can revoke one without breaking the others.

steps.title: Revoke a key you no longer need
steps.content:
1. Find the key in the table on the API Keys page.
  2. Click the delete (trash) icon in its row.
  3. Confirm. The key stops working immediately — any integration still using it will lose access, so update it first.

Check the Last Used column to spot keys that are no longer active before removing them.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaDescription: Generate, name, store, and revoke iMash API keys for programmatic REST API access under Account → API Keys. Keys are shown in full only once.
